methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $125M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Mythic is a Series D semiconductor company that has raised $125M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers in chips and semiconductors, with particular relevance to SambaNova Systems, Tenstorrent, d-Matrix, and DEEPX.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yielding $188M based on total capital raised as a proxy. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies produces $2.4B, anchored by SambaNova at $5.0B, Tenstorrent at $2.7B, d-Matrix at $2.0B, and DEEPX at $529M. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales (20.3x median across three public semiconductors) cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure for the target. 

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $159M (low) to $2.7B (high), with a median of $1.3B across both methods. The wide spread reflects genuine uncertainty: stage-progression yields a substantially lower figure, while peer-median reflects the elevated valuations observed in frontier semiconductor startups. Key uncertainties include Mythic's actual revenue run rate, competitive positioning relative to named peers, and whether recent private-market corrections in this sector apply to this cohort. Resolution of these factors would narrow the range considerably.
